manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: back, scooter, ankle, cottonmouth, head, bus, rat, rv, anaconda.
Your opponent's team words are: copper, fist, titanium, sheep, horse, grenade, steel, bronze.
Distractor words are: car, trailer, aluminum, oxygen, cave, dill, sage.
The kill words are: walnut.

message.content:
CLUE: Vehicle
TARGETS: bus, scooter, rv
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue related to some of our team's words: back, scooter, ankle, cottonmouth, head, bus, rat (maybe rat?), rv, anaconda. Choose clue that relates to multiple. For example "snake" could relate to anaconda, cottonmouth (both snakes). That's two. Maybe "transport" could relate to bus, scooter, rv. That's three. But must not be part of any listed words. "Transport" is okay. Could clue to bus, scooter, rv. That's three. Also "vehicle" could be bus, scooter, rv. But "vehicle" is safe. Might also connect to back? Not really. Let's think: "reptile" connects anaconda and cottonmouth. That's two. "road" connects bus, scooter, rv. So maybe choose one clue that captures three to maximize. "Road" is okay. Does it also hint at rat? Rats travel on roads? Not strong. "Travel" could connect bus, scooter, rv. Also maybe "back" as in "rear"? Not.

We need one clue word and list of target words that we are linking. Provide as many as we think partner can guess. Simpler: "Vehicle" -> bus, scooter, rv. That's three. Provide list: bus, scooter, rv.

Make sure clue not in any list. "Vehicle" not present. Good.

Return exactly format.
